zh.Bridlington, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾布利德林頓
zh.lifeSummary: William Marshall Anderson於1600年出生在英國越可爾布利德林頓。他於1632年與Eliza McArthur在英國越可爾布利德林頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1621年在英國英格蘭越可爾享年21歲，埋葬在英國越可爾布利德林頓。

zh-hans.Bridlington, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾布利德林頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Marshall Anderson于1600年出生在英国越可爾布利德林頓。他于1632年与Eliza McArthur在英国越可爾布利德林頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1621年在英国英格兰越可爾享年21岁，埋葬在英国越可爾布利德林頓。

nameMeanings.name: Anderson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern English: patronymic from the personal name Ander(s), a northern Middle English form of Andrew , + son ‘son’. The frequency of the surname in Scotland is attributable, at least in part, to the fact that Saint Andrew is the patron saint of Scotland, so the personal name has long enjoyed great popularity there. Legend has it that the saint's relics were taken to Scotland in the 4th century by a certain Saint Regulus. In North America, this surname has absorbed many cognate or like-sounding surnames in other languages, notably Scandinavian (see 3 and 4 below), but also Ukrainian Andreychenko etc.
German: patronymic from the personal name   Anders  , hence a cognate of 1 above.
Americanized form (and a less common Swedish variant) of Swedish   Andersson  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Andrew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the usual vernacular English form (recorded from the 13th century onward) of the New Testament Greek personal name Andreas . In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech Ondřej (see Ondrey ) and Albanian Ndreu , and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see examples at Andrews ).
History: The surname Andrew was first brought to North America from England by Robert Andrew (died 1668), who settled in Boxford, MA.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Marshall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, derived from a Norman French occupational term that originally denoted someone who looked after horses, ultimately from Germanic marah ‘horse’ + scalc ‘servant’. By the time it became fixed as a surname it had the meaning ‘shoeing smith’ later it came to denote an official whose duties were to a large extent ceremonial. The surname is pronounced the same as the Latin name Martial (from Latin Mars, genitive Martis; compare Martin ). This may have contributed something to its use as a given name.

meanings: Transferred use of the surname, derived from a Norman French occupational term that originally denoted someone who looked after horses, ultimately from Germanic marah ‘horse’ + scalc ‘servant’. historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: William Marshall Anderson was born in 1600, in Bridlington, Yorkshire, England. He married Eliza McArthur in 1632, in Bridlington, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1621, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 21, and was buried in Bridlington, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: William Marshall Anderson est né en 1600, à Bridlington, Everwyck, Angleterre. Il a épousé Eliza McArthur en 1632, à Bridlington, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1621, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 21 ans, et a été enterré à Bridlington, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: William Marshall Anderson wurde 1600 in Bridlington, Yorkshire, England geboren. Er hat 1632 in Bridlington, Yorkshire, England Eliza McArthur geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1621 im Alter von 21 Jahren in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Bridlington, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: William Marshall Anderson è nato il nel 1600 nel Bridlington, Yorkshire, England. Lui sposato Eliza McArthur nel 1632, nel Bridlington, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1621, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 21 anni, e fu sepolto a Bridlington, Yorkshire, England.

ja.Yorkshire, England: イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア
ja.Bridlington, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアブリドリントン
ja.lifeSummary: William Marshall Andersonは1600年にイギリスヨークシュアイアブリドリントンで生まれた。1632年にイギリスヨークシュアイアブリドリントンでEliza McArthurと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1621年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアで21歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアブリドリントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Marshall Anderson(은)는 1600년 영국 요르크실르 브리들링턴에서 태어났다. 그는 1632년 영국 요르크실르 브리들링턴에서 Eliza McArthur(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1621년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 21세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 브리들링턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Marshall Anderson nasceu em 1600, em Bridlington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Eliza McArthur em 1632, em Bridlington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1621, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 21 anos, e foi sepultado em Bridlington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: William Marshall Anderson родился в 1600 г. в местности бридлингтон, еркшир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Eliza McArthur в 1632 г. в местности бридлингтон, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1621 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 21 год и был похоронен в местности бридлингтон, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: William Marshall Anderson nació en 1600, en Bridlington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Eliza McArthur en 1632, en Bridlington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1621, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 21 años, y fue sepultado en Bridlington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
